calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patricia Mayer appeared by telephone for Jeff Altman (Petitioner).

Ms. Mayer addressed the issue of the non-court approved advanced withdrawals stating that there was a trust contest against the Successor Trustee who removed about $800,000.00 from the trust, that they put the probate matter on hold and that they did not touch base about the probate matter until after the trust matter was done. Ms. Mayer also addressed the issue of notice to Danielle Harden stating that she remembers doing an online search, that she sent notice to both addresses and that the notice that went to Colorado was just returned as a wrong address. In addition, Ms. Mayer stated that she was unable to reconcile the $22.29 discrepancy in the accounting and that it is not worth it to her Client to do the accounting again. The Seventh Amended First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Feb-25-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Julia Wald for (petitioner) Jeff Altman. Jonathon Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Ferdon expressed concerns regarding the daughter not being represented, determining if the lost will can be used in court and stated that Mr. Altman will need to figure out if the court has everything it needs regarding the will. Ms. Wald reported that she has tried to call the daughter but has been unable to contact her and stated that if the trust was not revoked she is not sure why he would have revoked the will and suspects he thought everything was in the trust. Mr. Ferdon stated that the daughter would have to file a petition to administer the estate, that there is not enough information to enter the will as a lost will asked what the $300,000.00 personal property was and was told by Ms. Wald that it is a brokerage account. The petition for letters of administration with will annexed was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Murillo. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Feb-09-2015 Continued To Feb-23-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jan-26-2015 Continued To Feb-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Dec-31-2014 Continued To Jan-26-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Julia Wald for Jeff Altman (Petitioner).

Ms. Wald stated that she believes either the estate planning attorney or the decedent's mother had conversation with the decedent's daughter and that it is her understanding that the daughter said she found a holographic will but that the daughter never showed it to anybody. Ms. Wald also stated that not listing the Trustee as a beneficiary was an oversight, addressed the issue of presumption of revocation stating that the decedent had carefully done estate planning and definitely did not want his daughter to inherit. The petition for letters of administration was continued to January 26, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a declaration regarding status of the holographic will and a declaration from the estate planning attorney regarding the above.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
